grades.label: Market Position
grades.evidence: The filing states explicitly that "NVIDIA is the world leader in visual computing" and describes leading product brands (GeForce, Quadro, Tesla, GRID) with GPUs driving ~75% of revenue and strong growth in high-end GeForce and Tesla products, indicating a category-leading position and pricing/power in core markets.

grades.label: Moat Type & Strength
grades.evidence: The company points to its invention of CUDA, a large developer/ecosystem ("virtualized GPU" and platform focus), and "7,300 patent assets" and heavy R&D ("invested $12 billion"), implying an ecosystem/network advantage; however the filing also notes rapid technology change and competitive risks (e.g., Tegra smartphone declines), so the advantage is meaningful but contested.

grades.label: Market Quality
grades.evidence: The filing characterizes addressed markets as having long-term tailwinds: "Gaming is one of the largest entertainment industries," accelerated/datacenter computing and deep learning adoption are growing rapidly, and automotive is described as a growing market for computing — supporting a secular growth view.

grades.label: Leadership Quality
grades.evidence: Management shows clear strategic focus on four platforms and vertical integration ("transformed into a specialized platform company"), heavy R&D investment ("invested $12 billion" and strong IP portfolio), and solid financial execution (56.1% gross margin, net income 12.3%); capital allocation appears focused on reinvestment and equity incentives (proposal to amend equity plan) with no concerning signals in the excerpts provided.

factors.label: moat type: network effects + market position: dominant + market quality: secular growth
